title: Generative AI and Copyright Infringement: A Legal-Technical Analysis of AI Music Generation Systems Under 17 U.S.C. Title 17
abstract: arXiv:2606.26111v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) has enabled users to synthesize music with text prompts, combining copyrighted lyrics, AI-composed melodies, and synthetic vocals that imitate real artists. This paper examines the legal and technical dimensions of AI-based music creation (e.g., Google Gemini's music tools) under U.S. copyright law. We analyze whether a user who inputs one artist's protected lyrics into a GenAI system, directs it to use another artist's voice or style, publishes the resulting song, and monetizes it violates 17 U.S.C. Section 106's exclusive rights [3]. The analysis integrates Title 17 doctrine (rights of reproduction, derivative works, distribution), 17 U.S.C. Section 114's narrow sound recording protection [4], and the new voice-cloning laws emerging at the state level [20].   We argue that unauthorized lyric copying poses a high risk of infringement of the musical composition, whereas mere AI-generated voice

translated_title: 生成式人工智能与版权侵权：基于美国法典第17编第17章的人工智能音乐生成系统的法律-技术分析
translated_abstract: 生成式人工智能（GenAI）使得用户能够通过文本提示合成音乐，将受版权保护的歌词、AI创作的旋律以及模仿真实艺术家的合成声音结合在一起。本文探讨了在美国版权法框架下，基于AI的音乐创作（例如谷歌Gemini的音乐工具）的法律与技术维度。我们分析了用户将一位艺术家的受保护歌词输入GenAI系统，指示其使用另一位艺术家的声音或风格，发布由此产生的歌曲并从中获利的行为，是否违反了美国法典第17编第106条所规定的专有权利。该分析整合了第17编的原则（复制权、衍生作品权、发行权）、第17编第114条对录音作品的狭窄保护，以及各州新兴的声音克隆法律。我们认为，未经授权复制歌词对音乐作品的侵权风险较高，而仅仅是AI生成的声音则风险较低。
tldr: 本文分析指出，在生成式AI音乐系统中，未经授权复制歌词构成高风险的版权侵权，而单纯模仿艺术家声音的AI生成行为则风险较低。
en_tdlr: This paper analyzes that unauthorized copying of lyrics in generative AI music systems poses a high risk of copyright infringement, while mere AI-generated imitation of an artist's voice poses a lower risk.
